title: Why a Cancer Drug That Restores Differentiation Is Better Than One That Kills
color_semantics: TEAL = differentiated/mature cell/drug working/epigenetic lock released; CRIMSON = immature blast/2HG present/epigenetic lock engaged

metadata.note: EXCLUSIONS: no IDH biochemistry beyond 2HG-as-competitive-inhibitor concept; no comparison of IDH1 vs IDH2 inhibitors; no distinction between AML and glioma IDH mutations; no DNMT inhibitor mechanism; no discussion of EZH2 inhibitors
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Wonder register (voices/wonder/VOICE.md + MEDHAVY.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the MEDHAVY outro from AUTHOR.MD :: Medhavy.com
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: A leukemia patient starts a new drug. Weeks later the blast count falls. But here's the thing — the cancer cells did not die.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A leukemia patient starts a new drug. Weeks later the blast count falls. But here's the thing — the cancer cells did not die.

props.headline: On the blood smear: mature neutrophils with segmented nuclei — cells…

beats.narration_text: On the blood smear: mature neutrophils with segmented nuclei — cells that should not exist in this bone marrow.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: On the blood smear: mature neutrophils with segmented nuclei — cells that should not exist in this bone marrow.

beats.narration_text: Those neutrophils carry the original IDH1 mutation in their genome. They were the cancer. They grew up.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Those neutrophils carry the original IDH1 mutation in their genome. They were the cancer. They grew up.

beats.narration_text: Cancer drugs are supposed to kill cancer cells. Here blast counts fall but the cells do not die — they mature. How does blocking a metabolic enzyme produce cells that finish differentiating?

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Cancer drugs are supposed to kill cancer cells. Here blast counts fall but the cells do not die — they mature. How does blocking a metabolic enzyme produce cell

beats.narration_text: Normal blood development runs one direction: a stem cell commits, matures through stages, and eventually dies of old age. The mature cell is the endpoint — that process is always running in healthy bone marrow.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Normal blood development runs one direction: a stem cell commits, matures through stages, and eventually dies of old age. The mature cell is the endpoint — that

beats.narration_text: In AML, that program stalls. Blasts — immature progenitors — accumulate because the differentiation gate is locked shut. The program is intact. It just cannot run.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: In AML, that program stalls. Blasts — immature progenitors — accumulate because the differentiation gate is locked shut. The program is intact. It just cannot r

beats.narration_text: Here is why the IDH1 mutation locks it. The mutation produces a metabolite called 2-hydroxyglutarate — 2HG. It closely resembles the molecule that demethylases need to erase methyl marks from DNA.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Here is why the IDH1 mutation locks it. The mutation produces a metabolite called 2-hydroxyglutarate — 2HG. It closely resembles the molecule that demethylases

beats.narration_text: 2HG jams the demethylases by competitive inhibition. Methyl marks that should be erased to open differentiation genes stay locked in place. The epigenetic lock holds — and the blast cannot mature.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: 2HG jams the demethylases by competitive inhibition. Methyl marks that should be erased to open differentiation genes stay locked in place. The epigenetic lock

beats.narration_text: Ivosidenib blocks the mutant IDH1 enzyme. 2HG levels fall. Demethylases recover. The methyl marks on differentiation genes are gradually erased — and the gate begins to open.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Ivosidenib blocks the mutant IDH1 enzyme. 2HG levels fall. Demethylases recover. The methyl marks on differentiation genes are gradually erased — and the gate b

props.headline: The cell was always going to differentiate. The program was intact.…

beats.narration_text: The interesting part is this: the cell was always going to differentiate. The developmental program was intact. Only a chemical lock — 2HG — was preventing it from running.
Illustrative: day 0 — eighty percent blasts. Day 14 — bilobed nuclei appear. Day 28 — mature neutrophils refill the marrow. Day 56 — fewer than five percent blasts remain.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Illustrative: day 0 — eighty percent blasts. Day 14 — bilobed nuclei appear. Day 28 — mature neutrophils refill the marrow. Day 56 — fewer than five percent bla

beats.narration_text: A metabolic drug removed a chemical lock, and a developmental program ran to completion. The cancer cells did not die. They finished growing up.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A metabolic drug removed a chemical lock, and a developmental program ran to completion. The cancer cells did not die. They finished growing up.
